apsNote: Health Sciences closes 30 June. NBT required for most faculties.
requiredDocuments: Certified copy of South African ID or valid passport
Certified copy of matric certificate or statement of results
Proof of residence (not older than 3 months)

internationalStudents.note: International applicants require SAQA evaluation of qualifications.

minimumAPS.note: UCT uses Faculty Points Score /600 for competitive entry

apsSystem: APS /600 (sum of 6 best NSC subject percentages, excluding Life Orientation; results below 40% score zero)

flags: healthSciencesCloses: no separate earlier closing date for Health Sciences confirmed from official UCT sources — same 31 July 2026 deadline applies; third-party sources suggested NBT deadline differs but not application close date

minApsRange: expressed as APS/600 (UCT uses percentage-based scoring out of 600, not the standard /42 APS); range estimates based on third-party aggregator data cross-referenced with official subject requirements since official UCT site does not publish consolidated APS tables

programme APS values sourced from third-party aggregator (studentroom.co.za) cross-checked against official subject requirements; UCT official site directs to faculty-specific PDFs for exact cut-offs — treat as indicative only
MBChB aps:450 is a weighted score approximation; UCT uses a complex weighted formula for Health Sciences, not a simple APS sum
students: ~30,000 based on 2023 data from UCT News (17,862 UG + 11,638 PG + 465 occasional)
